message:
fix: harden Streamdown inline tokenizer against empty-content tokens

Add minimum-length guards to each token type in tokenizeInline() to
prevent rendering empty React elements from degenerate markdown like
a lone backtick or bare asterisks:

- Inline code: require token.length > 2 (needs at least one char
  between backticks) before rendering <code>
- Bold-italic: require token.length > 6 (at least one char between
  triple asterisks) before rendering <strong><em>
- Bold: require token.length > 4 (at least one char between double
  asterisks) before rendering <strong>
- Italic: require token.length > 2 (at least one char between single
  asterisks) before rendering <em>

For links, add early validation that both label and hrefRaw are
non-empty before calling sanitizeHref(), avoiding unnecessary URL
sanitization work on malformed tokens. Restructure the link branch
to check emptiness first, then sanitize, keeping the happy path
indented one level deeper but more defensively correct.

Update inline comments above the regex to note the "must have content"
constraint for each token group.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
